meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Project Management to Solve 'Stuck' App Building
content.seo_description: Break out of tutorial hell. Learn expert project management strategies using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade AI applications.
content.answer_block: You are stuck because you lack a production-grade workflow. To ship real-world apps, you must transition from coding in a vacuum to managing modular, scalable features. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for iterative execution, you convert abstract ideas into concrete, deployable assets through structured project management.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks in tutorial purgatory, watching endless videos while your own IDE remains a graveyard of half-finished 'To-Do' lists and broken API integrations. The flicker of excitement you felt when writing your first 'Hello World' has been replaced by the crushing weight of scope creep and architectural paralysis. It’s not a lack of talent; it’s a lack of a battle-tested framework.

Today, the cycle ends. We aren't just writing code; we are engineering a system. By treating your student project like a professional SaaS launch, you gain the clarity needed to push past the 'stuck' phase. We will use Antigravity to map your logic and Claude Code to turn those blueprints into high-velocity implementation, effectively turning your laptop into a senior-level dev shop.

steps.title: Define the Atomic MVP
steps.description: Stop building features for a hypothetical million users. Focus on one single, high-value interaction path that proves your core hypothesis.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project idea and distill it into a single, atomic MVP feature. Generate a user flow diagram that prioritizes the core value proposition and excludes all 'nice-to-have' bloat.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a project roadmap file listing only the 3 essential tasks for the MVP'

steps.title: Architect the Data Flow
steps.description: Before writing code, map how state moves through your app. This prevents the 'spaghetti code' that keeps beginners stuck.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a data flow schema for my MVP. Define the state management requirements between the frontend, the AI processing layer, and the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a schema.json file representing the core data structures based on the antigravity architecture'

steps.title: Component-Driven Implementation
steps.description: Build in isolation. Use Claude Code to generate modular components that can be tested independently of the main application state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break down the MVP into 5 independent, testable components. Provide the interface contracts for each component to ensure they communicate correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate the component boilerplate for the authentication and primary dashboard modules'

steps.title: Automated Testing Loop
steps.description: Don't manually refresh your browser to check for errors. Build a loop where Claude Code automatically writes and runs tests for every new feature.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a test suite strategy for my MVP. Identify the critical paths that must have automated unit tests to ensure stability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'setup jest configuration and generate unit tests for the primary API utility functions'

steps.title: Continuous Deployment Path
steps.description: A project isn't real until it's deployed. Automate the handoff from your local environment to a cloud provider.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment checklist for a Vercel/Supabase stack. Provide the necessary environment variable configurations and build scripts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a github actions workflow file for automated deployment on push to main'

pro_tips: Treat every bug as a feature request for a better abstraction layer, not just a line to fix.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write it; 'self-documenting' is a myth for beginners.
Limit your daily active development to 3 hours to prevent burnout and maintain decision-making clarity.
Never merge code that hasn't been validated by a unit test, even if it 'looks right' in the browser.
Keep a 'Decision Log' markdown file to document why you chose specific libraries or architectures; it's gold for job interviews.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus on gathering user feedback. Use the same Claude Code workflow to implement one user-requested feature per week to build consistent momentum.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the roadmap, and the expert methodology to stop spinning your wheels and start shipping software that matters. Don't let another weekend slip away in tutorial hell. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
